claim_text: Mudra loan scheme ke under humne 40 crore se zyada loans diye hain, jisse log self-employed ban rahe hain.

card.claim_text: Mudra loan scheme ke under humne 40 crore se zyada loans diye hain, jisse log self-employed ban rahe hain.
card.confidence_reason: Relying on official PIB and ministry data confirming the count of over 40 crore disbursements.

grounded_context.point: PIB reports over 43 crore Mudra loans have been sanctioned as of FY24 [1].
Official ministry stats show ₹24 lakh crore has been disbursed to foster credit access [2].

card.source_disagreement: None. Government sources do not report operational criticisms or small average sizes.

card.claim_text: Mudra loan scheme ke under humne 40 crore se zyada loans diye hain, jisse log self-employed ban rahe hain.
card.confidence_reason: News reports cover both government figures and critiques of loan sizes.

grounded_context.point: Mainstream news outlets report that 40+ crore loans were sanctioned under the scheme [1].
Reports quote economists warning that the average loan size is small and may not generate long-term hiring [2].

missing_context: News articles point out that a large percentage of self-employed micro-ventures shut down within the first two years.
card.source_disagreement: Disagreement highlighted between government employment claims and independent banking analyses.

sources_used.title: The Impact of Mudra Loans - Indian Express

card.claim_text: Mudra loan scheme ke under humne 40 crore se zyada loans diye hain, jisse log self-employed ban rahe hain.
card.confidence_reason: Fact-checking agencies provide detailed breakdowns of the Shishu loan category dominance.

grounded_context.point: Fact-checks verify the count of 40 crore loans but highlight that over 83% of them are 'Shishu' loans (under ₹50,000) [1].
Average loan size across all Mudra disbursements is ₹62,000, raising doubts about their ability to create sustainable payroll jobs [2].

missing_context: A massive portion of the credit is allocated to micro-entities for subsistence-level working capital rather than job-creating capital investments.
card.source_disagreement: Clear methodological disagreement. The government counts credit accounts as jobs, whereas labor economists classify them as subsistence self-employment.

sources_used.title: Analyzing Mudra Loan Job Claims - BOOM Fact Check

card.claim_text: Mudra loan scheme ke under humne 40 crore se zyada loans diye hain, jisse log self-employed ban rahe hain.
card.confidence_reason: Mixed sources allow a balanced synthesis of official counts and structural context.

grounded_context.point: Over 43 crore loans worth ₹24 lakh crore have been sanctioned [1].
Over 80% of these disbursements are under ₹50,000 (Shishu category) [2].

missing_context: Small loan sizes limit the scale of business expansion and job creation.
card.source_disagreement: Government statistics emphasize total credit reach, while independent economists focus on loan size adequacy.

claim_text: Desh mein highway construction ki speed 37 kilometers per day tak pahunch gayi hai, jo pichle sarkar se teen guna hai.

card.claim_text: Desh mein highway construction ki speed 37 kilometers per day tak pahunch gayi hai, jo pichle sarkar se teen guna hai.
card.confidence_reason: Based directly on MoRTH reports documenting the 37 km/day peak rate in FY21.

grounded_context.point: Ministry reports show highway construction speed reached a peak of 37 km/day during FY21, compared to around 12-16 km/day under the previous administration [1].

card.source_disagreement: None. Official government sources do not document the change in measurement methodology.

card.claim_text: Desh mein highway construction ki speed 37 kilometers per day tak pahunch gayi hai, jo pichle sarkar se teen guna hai.
card.confidence_reason: Mixed sources successfully capture the speed stats and the lane-kilometer methodology context.

grounded_context.point: Peak construction speed reached 37 km/day in FY21 [1].
Methodology changed from linear length to lane-kilometers in 2018, distorting direct historical comparisons [2].

missing_context: Direct comparison to pre-2018 speed is distorted by the lane-kilometer measurement shift.
card.source_disagreement: Ministry reports emphasize lane-kilometer speed, while fact-checkers point out the false historical comparison.

card.claim_text: Desh mein highway construction ki speed 37 kilometers per day tak pahunch gayi hai, jo pichle sarkar se teen guna hai.
card.confidence_reason: Fact-checkers explain the 2018 methodology shift that inflate post-2018 construction speeds.

grounded_context.point: MoRTH achieved a 37 km/day peak rate in FY21, but the subsequent average has settled between 28 and 32 km/day [1].
In 2018, the ministry changed its metric from 'linear length' to 'lane kilometers' (multiplying physical length by number of lanes built) [2].

missing_context: Comparing linear kilometers (pre-2018) to lane kilometers (post-2018) is a false comparison, as multi-lane highways constructed post-2018 appear multi-fold faster than the old linear metric would indicate.
card.source_disagreement: Disagreement between raw ministry speed claims and methodological fact-checks.

sources_used.title: Highway Length Measurement Shift - MoRTH Circular
Is Highway Speed Really Three Times Faster? - Factly

card.claim_text: Desh mein highway construction ki speed 37 kilometers per day tak pahunch gayi hai, jo pichle sarkar se teen guna hai.
card.confidence_reason: Mixed sources successfully capture the speed stats and the lane-kilometer methodology context.

grounded_context.point: Peak construction speed reached 37 km/day in FY21 [1].
Methodology changed from linear length to lane-kilometers in 2018, distorting direct historical comparisons [2].

missing_context: Direct comparison to pre-2018 speed is distorted by the lane-kilometer measurement shift.
card.source_disagreement: Ministry reports emphasize lane-kilometer speed, while fact-checkers point out the false historical comparison.

claim_text: Desh mein pichle 45 saal mein sabse zyada unemployment rate aaj hai, PLFS data isko proof karta hai.

card.claim_text: Desh mein pichle 45 saal mein sabse zyada unemployment rate aaj hai, PLFS data isko proof karta hai.
card.confidence_reason: Relying on official PLFS annual trends showing a decline in unemployment.

grounded_context.point: Annual PLFS surveys show that the unemployment rate has steadily declined from 6.1% in 2017-18 to 3.2% in 2022-23 [1].

missing_context: The claim of a '45-year high' is outdated and ignores subsequent years of official survey data.
card.source_disagreement: None on the official numbers, but government reports do not mention independent employment metrics.

sources_used.title: Annual Report Periodic Labour Force Survey - MoSPI

card.claim_text: Desh mein pichle 45 saal mein sabse zyada unemployment rate aaj hai, PLFS data isko proof karta hai.
card.confidence_reason: Balanced synthesis showing both the historical 2017-18 peak and the subsequent declining trend.

grounded_context.point: The 2017-18 PLFS recorded a 45-year high unemployment rate of 6.1% [1].
Subsequent annual surveys show a declining trend, reaching 3.2% in 2022-23 [2].

missing_context: The 45-year high statistic is outdated and does not reflect current annual survey results.
card.source_disagreement: Political debates focus on the 2017-18 peak, while official data tracks the declining trend.

card.claim_text: Desh mein pichle 45 saal mein sabse zyada unemployment rate aaj hai, PLFS data isko proof karta hai.
card.confidence_reason: Fact-checks clarify that the 45-year high refers specifically to the 2017-18 survey year.

grounded_context.point: The 2017-18 PLFS survey recorded a 45-year high unemployment rate of 6.1% [1].
However, subsequent PLFS surveys report a decline to 3.2% in 2022-23 [2].

missing_context: The speaker is presenting a historical 2017-18 data point as the current ('aaj') unemployment rate, omitting five subsequent years of declining statistics.
card.source_disagreement: Fact-checkers verify that the 6.1% was a 45-year high, but clarify that using it as a present-day statistic is misleading.

sources_used.title: Unemployment Rate in India Fact Check - BOOM Fact Check

card.claim_text: Desh mein pichle 45 saal mein sabse zyada unemployment rate aaj hai, PLFS data isko proof karta hai.
card.confidence_reason: Balanced synthesis showing both the historical 2017-18 peak and the subsequent declining trend.

grounded_context.point: The 2017-18 PLFS recorded a 45-year high unemployment rate of 6.1% [1].
Subsequent annual surveys show a declining trend, reaching 3.2% in 2022-23 [2].

missing_context: The 45-year high statistic is outdated and does not reflect current annual survey results.
card.source_disagreement: Political debates focus on the 2017-18 peak, while official data tracks the declining trend.
